concerns.text: FR-005 mandates a dynamic block-length adjustment (reduce to 1×bin_size if independent blocks < 30). T023 defines a fixed block length of 2×bin_size without the conditional logic. T028 describes the logic but is a separate task; the implementation of the core bootstrap algorithm (T023) is incomplete without the fallback, risking a violation of the statistical protocol if the dataset is short.

FR-011 requires the system to VERIFY that false-positive rate ≤ 0.05 and power ≥ 0.8. T029 records metrics to a report, but no task exists to explicitly EXECUTE the verification check against these thresholds and fail the build if they are not met. The success criterion (SC-008) is unenforced by the task list.

FR-010 restricts bin sizes to integers between 7 and 60 days inclusive. T040 mentions 'validating bin size range' but does not explicitly mandate the enforcement of the 7–60 day hard limits or the rejection of invalid inputs. The 'configurable' requirement's safety constraints are not fully covered.

FR-002 requires exponential backoff for NOAA downloads. T006 implements this logic in the foundational phase. T018 redundantly re-implements 'retry logic' for the same source in Phase 3. This creates conflicting implementation paths and potential logic drift. T018 should be removed or merged into T006.

T017.1 describes 'partial data recovery logic' to switch to Auger-only mode. The spec's US-1 Acceptance Scenario 2 only requires logging a warning and proceeding with available data (≥90% rows), not a specific 'switch mode' logic. T017.1 may be over-engineering a simple fallback that could be handled by existing error handling in T014.
